title: COBOL to Python Banking Backbone Migration with Provable Functional Equivalence
system_context: You are part of a team of 3 AI agents planning the migration of a COBOL banking backbone (transaction processing, account management, batch settlement) to Python. The migration must maintain provable functional equivalence -- every COBOL program must produce identical outputs for identical inputs after porting. The existing COBOL system processes 2 million transactions daily, has 500+ COBOL programs, and runs on an IBM z/OS mainframe. Discuss: symmetric translation strategy (function-by-function porting), LLM-based validation testing of ported code vs original, automated test generation from production traces, error detection heuristics, rollback strategy, and phased cutover plan. Each agent has a specific role: Architect (migration strategy and phased plan), Developer (tooling for automated translation and testing), Reviewer (risk analysis and compliance concerns).
initial_prompt: Plan the migration of a COBOL banking backbone to Python. Cover: translation strategy, test generation, functional equivalence verification, phased cutover, and risk mitigation.
